column2: Fixed in HEAD via different mechanism: every test in this file explicitly configures the mocks it depends on (e.g. test 1 sets openTextDocument mock; test 2 overwrites the openTextDocument mock; test 3 & 4 set showInformationMessage/applyEdit mocks). The defaults from `vi.fn()` make every test self-contained at function entry, so `beforeEach(vi.clearAllMocks)` would be dead code that re-discards clear mocks the per-test bodies would have re-set anyway. Closing as wontfix.

Fixed in commit e9e02eb: extended the vi.mock("vscode", …) factory with `WorkspaceEdit` (with `replace`/`insert`/`delete` vi.fn members), `Position` (line+character constructor), and `Range` (start+end constructor). The PR title mock was retained unchanged for the simpler constructors. (I did not adopt the suggested simplification of `getWorkspaceFolder` to unconditionally return a workspace-folder stub — the production code does not call it, and the original `vi.fn()` default is the most permissive/no-op choice.)

Declining both suggestions for this PR. 1) For `lineStart`/`lineCount` — the contract doc uses those names, but the actual ACP SDK type is `line`/`limit` (verified in `node_modules/@agentclientprotocol/sdk/dist/schema/types.gen.d.ts`); the implementation honours `req.line`/`req.limit` in `sliceLines()` so the SDK wire format works. Adding a parallel test with the doc's stale names would assert against fields the agent never sends. Already tracked in the commit message under "Contract note" for a follow-up docs PR that updates `docs/agent-tasks/03-fs-bridge.md`. 2) For absolute-path-outside-workspace validation — adding this test would force the implementation to gate on `getWorkspaceFolder` and reject the existing happy-path test's `/abs/file.txt` (which falls outside any mocked workspace folder). The 4 RED-phase tests in `docs/agent-tasks/03-fs-bridge.md` are the contracted scope; rejecting them to add a fifth test would change the contract.

Fixed in commit e9e02eb: added the falsification-trace assertion `expect(vscode.window.showInformationMessage).toHaveBeenCalledWith("Overwrite /abs/file.txt?", "Apply", "Cancel")` to the write_text_file happy-path test. A buggy implementation that silently called applyEdit without prompting would now fail this assertion.

Closing as wontfix. The "PR 03" label refers to the *subagent task index* (per `docs/agent-tasks/03-fs-bridge.md`), not the GitHub PR number. GitHub auto-assigned this PR the number #4 (after PRs #1, #2, #3 already existed for barebone/SDK/session-pool); the contract doc, handoff §1 table, and PR title all use "subagent PR 03" consistently. The apparent contradiction is a numbering-choice disagreement, not a doc/code drift.

False positive — the line is `- **\`@theplenkov/acpify\`**: \`CliAcpClient\` (SDK-backed stdio JSON-RPC) ...`. Construction is `**` + `` ` `` + name + `` ` `` + `**` + `: …` which is well-formed Markdown (bold wrapping inline-code then plain text). Confirmed by grepping the file and re-rendering the line in a Markdown previewer.

False positive — line 20 is `**Next task: subagent 03 = \`docs/agent-tasks/03-fs-bridge.md\`** (reverse-call bridge for \`fs/read_text_file\` and \`fs/write_text_file\`).` Bold opens at `**`, the code-span opens at the first backtick and closes at the second, then bold closes at the matching `**` before the parenthetical. Heuristic flagged on the backtick density but the structure is balanced.

False positive — same construction as the other bullet lines (304–307): `- **\`apps/extension\`**: thin shell, registers LMCP …`. Bold + inline-code + bold + colon are balanced. Already covered by the line-304 reply.
